current_behavior: Runtime semantics keeps local aliases, plain user-function by-reference parameters, local/static-local by-reference returns, array-element references, by-reference closure captures, and local-array by-reference foreach executable while classifying property aliases, globals, and temporary by-reference foreach sources with stable gaps
reference_behavior: PHP aliases variables, parameters, returns, array elements, foreach values, and object properties through engine reference/COW storage

current_behavior: Runtime semantics seeds controlled CLI `$argc`, `$argv`, `$_SERVER['argc']`, `$_SERVER['argv']`, sorted explicit `$_ENV`, empty request-style arrays, and fixture-covered `global $x`/`$GLOBALS` aliases; no host environment is imported implicitly
feature: Complete superglobal and SAPI request matrix
reference_behavior: PHP populates `$_SERVER`, `$_ENV`, `$_GET`, `$_POST`, `$_COOKIE`, `$_FILES`, `$_REQUEST`, `$GLOBALS`, `$argc`, and `$argv` from CLI, INI, environment, request, and SAPI state

current_behavior: Runtime semantics covers direct global aliases and direct `$GLOBALS["name"]` read/write fixtures. Dynamic global variables, direct `$GLOBALS[]` append/proxy edge cases, and the full SAPI interaction matrix remain deferred with the reference-semantics known gaps
reference_behavior: PHP `$GLOBALS` is the live global symbol table and array entries alias corresponding globals

current_behavior: Runtime semantics lowers free generator functions, returns generator objects lazily, and executes by-value `foreach` over the covered generator MVP. `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_GENERATOR` remains documented as the historical Runtime diagnostic and for still-unsupported generator methods/closures beyond the MVP.
historical_diagnostic_reason: Historical diagnostic retained for compatibility documentation while fixture-covered behavior is implemented.
reference_behavior: PHP executes generator functions lazily and supports `foreach` iteration

current_behavior: Runtime semantics executes `yield from` over arrays and generator MVP objects, including delegated keys/values and covered return-value propagation. `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_YIELD_FROM` remains documented as the historical Runtime diagnostic and for unsupported delegation sources outside the Runtime semantics MVP.
historical_diagnostic_reason: Historical diagnostic retained for compatibility documentation while fixture-covered behavior is implemented.
reference_behavior: PHP delegates iteration to another generator/Traversable/array and propagates generator return semantics

current_behavior: Runtime semantics executes internal Fiber objects, covered lifecycle methods, suspend/resume/throw/getReturn continuations, and invalid transition diagnostics. `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_FIBER` remains documented as the historical Runtime diagnostic for unsupported Fiber forms outside the Runtime semantics MVP.
historical_diagnostic_reason: Historical diagnostic retained for compatibility documentation while fixture-covered behavior is implemented.
reference_behavior: PHP executes `Fiber` objects with suspend/resume scheduling semantics

current_behavior: Runtime semantics lowers `eval` to a VM instruction and executes fixture-covered statement/expression eval with shared locals, return values, and parse/compile/runtime diagnostics. `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_EVAL` remains documented as the historical Runtime diagnostic; eval declarations remain `E_PHP_VM_EVAL_DECLARATION_GAP`.
historical_diagnostic_reason: Historical diagnostic retained for compatibility documentation while fixture-covered behavior is implemented.
reference_behavior: PHP parses and executes runtime source text in the current scope

current_behavior: Runtime semantics registers request-local SPL autoload callbacks, invokes them during unresolved class/interface lookup, supports include-backed definitions, and preserves recursive-autoload guards. The historical `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_AUTOLOAD` diagnostic remains documented for Runtime; Composer path conventions and the wider SPL autoload API remain covered by narrower autoload/runtime rows.
historical_diagnostic_reason: Historical diagnostic retained for compatibility documentation while fixture-covered autoload behavior is implemented.
reference_behavior: PHP invokes registered autoload callbacks during class/interface/trait/enum lookup

current_behavior: stable unsupported diagnostic
reference_behavior: PHP models resources, stream wrappers, filters, and stream metadata

current_behavior: Work item permits only canonical files below configured roots; outside-root paths produce deterministic diagnostics
reference_behavior: PHP resolves paths through include_path, cwd, stream wrappers, and filesystem permissions

current_behavior: VM resolves relative to the including file only; missing `include` records a warning and continues with `false`, while missing `require` is fatal
feature: Full PHP include/require warning text and include_path search
reference_behavior: PHP searches include_path and emits engine warning/fatal wording

current_behavior: Work item shares matching local slots for current top-level fixture programs; cross-file symbol merging and redeclaration behavior are deferred
feature: Complete include scope and symbol side effects
reference_behavior: PHP executes included code in the current scope and may define functions, classes, and constants

current_behavior: stable unsupported diagnostic outside the selected builtin registry
feature: Full PHP standard library and extensions
reference_behavior: PHP exposes the full standard library and loaded extensions with engine-specific arity/type behavior

current_behavior: Runtime semantics Work item executes fixture-covered trait method composition, precedence, aliases, visibility changes, conflicts, private methods, and origin metadata. The historical `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_TRAIT_RUNTIME` diagnostic remains documented for Runtime; remaining gaps are trait properties, constants, nested trait uses, exact diagnostics, and wider consistency checks.
reference_behavior: PHP composes trait methods/properties with aliasing, precedence, visibility changes, conflicts, and magic interactions

current_behavior: Runtime semantics Work item dispatches `__get`, `__set`, `__isset`, and `__unset` for fixture-covered missing/inaccessible property operations and guards recursive property magic. Work item dispatches `__call`, `__callStatic`, `__invoke`, and `__toString` for fixture-covered call and string-conversion paths, and reports `__debugInfo` as an explicit gap. Work item gives accessible declared property hooks priority before storage while keeping magic as the missing/inaccessible fallback. Clone serialization magic and by-reference `__get` lvalues remain explicit gaps.
reference_behavior: PHP invokes magic methods according to visibility, scope, and object-operation rules

current_behavior: Runtime semantics Work item creates a new object identity with a shallow property-map copy, invokes public instance `__clone` after the copy, propagates `__clone` exceptions, and applies public clone-with replacements to the clone after `__clone`. Work item dispatches fixture-covered public `set` hooks for clone-with replacements. Private/protected/readonly/static/full-property-hook clone-with replacement details remain explicit gaps.
feature: Remaining clone magic and clone-with edge cases
reference_behavior: PHP invokes `__clone` after object storage is copied and applies visibility/scope rules

current_behavior: Work item lowers only statically named non-nullsafe method calls such as `$obj->method()` and emits a stable diagnostic for wider method-call forms
reference_behavior: PHP resolves dynamic method names and nullsafe calls at runtime with visibility, magic-method, and nullable receiver semantics

current_behavior: Runtime semantics resolves fixture-covered core/platform constants through the standard-library registry, including PHP version components, integer-size constants, math constants, and path separators. Extension/build-specific constants outside the registry remain standard-library coverage work, not this runtime diagnostic.
reference_behavior: PHP exposes many core, extension, OS, and build-specific predefined constants

current_behavior: Runtime semantics executes `define()`, `defined()`, and `constant()` for scalar userland constants through the VM constant table and preserves duplicate-define false returns. Namespace and wider case-sensitivity edge cases remain standard compatibility work.
reference_behavior: PHP creates constants dynamically at runtime with namespace and case-sensitivity rules

current_behavior: Work item consumes folded Semantic frontend const-expression HIR values and does not evaluate arbitrary PHP expressions in the runtime
feature: Full runtime evaluation of constant expressions
reference_behavior: PHP accepts and evaluates the language's allowed constant-expression subset

current_behavior: Runtime semantics Work item executes the fixture-covered MVP: get/set hook dispatch through VM call frames, backed vs virtual metadata, asymmetric setter visibility checks, type checks, recursion diagnostics, magic fallback priority, and public clone-with set-hook replacement. The historical `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_PROPERTY_HOOKS` diagnostic remains documented for Runtime; full hook grammar, explicit hook parameter syntax, inheritance/override compatibility, readonly interaction matrices, by-reference lvalues, exact engine diagnostics, and parser support for defaults immediately before hook lists remain explicit gaps.
reference_behavior: PHP executes property hooks on reads/writes with visibility, backing value, inheritance, readonly, clone-with, and by-reference rules

current_behavior: stable unsupported diagnostic
reference_behavior: PHP enforces initialization, visibility, clone-with, and write-once rules for these properties

current_behavior: Work item applies replacements only to declared public properties after shallow cloning and checks simple public typed-property replacements. Work item adds fixture-covered public `set` hook dispatch during clone-with replacement. Unsupported private/protected/readonly/static/full-hook categories are classified instead of silently overwritten.
reference_behavior: PHP 8.5 clone-with honors visibility, readonly, property hooks, typed properties, and engine object initialization rules

current_behavior: Work item adds VM-internal `Exception`, `Error`, `TypeError`, `ValueError`, `ArgumentCountError`, and `FiberError` objects with public `message`, covered `Throwable`/`Error`/exact-class `instanceof`, and deterministic `E_PHP_VM_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION` diagnostics. Full stack trace formatting, codes, previous exceptions, and engine text parity remain gaps.
feature: Complete Throwable/Error/Exception hierarchy and stacktrace formatting
reference_behavior: PHP uses built-in `Throwable`, `Exception`, `Error`, stack traces, codes, previous exceptions, and engine-specific fatal output

current_behavior: Work item matches covered catch types against the VM-internal throwable hierarchy instead of catch-all behavior. The remaining exception gaps are complete multi-catch/multiple-catch-clause parity and exact engine diagnostics, tracked under the throwable/finally rows.
feature: Catch type forms outside the exception MVP
reference_behavior: PHP matches catch clauses against class/interface hierarchy and union catch types

current_behavior: Work item runs finally for normal try completion, return, thrown try-body control-flow, and nonmatching catches before outer handlers. Break/continue, destructor, generator, and fiber interaction matrices remain deferred.
feature: Complete finally interaction with catch-thrown control-flow, break/continue, destructors, generators, fibers, and nested handlers
reference_behavior: PHP runs finally for all abrupt control-flow paths and preserves precise pending control-flow semantics

current_behavior: Runtime semantics Work item executes the fixture-covered MVP: unit/backed enum metadata, singleton cases, `name`/`value`, `cases()`, `from()`, `tryFrom()`, identity, switch/match, methods, static methods, constants, trait-composed methods, and invalid extension/instantiation/value diagnostics. The historical `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_ENUM_RUNTIME` diagnostic remains documented for Runtime; serialization, ReflectionEnum public APIs, exact engine diagnostics, and the wider enum consistency matrix remain explicit gaps.
reference_behavior: PHP creates enum case singleton objects with class-like methods, properties, backed values, identity semantics, serialization rules, and Reflection metadata

current_behavior: Work items 26-28 expose attribute metadata and broaden the minimal Reflection metadata handles. `ReflectionClass('stdClass')` is executable through the supported fixture. The historical `E_PHP_IR_UNSUPPORTED_REFLECTION` diagnostic remains documented for Runtime; unsupported Reflection methods report `E_PHP_VM_UNKNOWN_METHOD`, unsupported callable reflection reports `E_PHP_VM_REFLECTION_UNSUPPORTED_CALLABLE`, and the wider class/function/attribute/enum API matrix remains explicit gap work.
feature: Reflection outside the Work item metadata MVP
reference_behavior: PHP exposes runtime metadata through Reflection classes and functions, including instantiation-sensitive attribute objects and full enum/class/function APIs

Warning wording, INF/NAN, resource/extension conversion/comparison cases, recursive array/object comparison edge cases, and full error-object compatibility remain deferred

current_behavior: Runtime semantics keeps array-to-string conversion as a stable diagnostic gap until the warning channel and exact output are modeled

current_behavior: Runtime semantics dispatches public instance `__toString` in string-cast, echo, concat, and formatting paths, propagates thrown exceptions, and reports deterministic errors for missing/inaccessible/non-string returns.

current_behavior: Work item supports int, string, bool, null, finite float truncation, and basic decimal integer-string keys only

reference_behavior: PHP unpacks arrays into literals with list/key merge behavior and runtime errors for invalid operands

current_behavior: Runtime semantics executes direct array-element references, COW separation, and by-value foreach snapshots over reference elements for covered fixtures; wider matrix cases remain explicit gaps
